- **Duração:** 02:14

---

## [00:00 - 02:14]

Here's a simple and profound exercise that will help you improve your communication skills. I want you to answer this question for me. Let's say you're at a bar and you're talking to a new group of people. When you leave that conversation, what three words would you want those people to use when they're describing you? When I did this about three years ago, I wrote down these following words. The first one is I wanted people to think I'm confident. The second one is I wanted people to think that I'm fun and playful. And then the third thing is I wanted people to think that I'm smart. These were the three that I picked. These words are very important because you have to start with the end in mind. Now that you have these words in mind and you have the end in mind, next look for someone in your circle or someone online who's confident and then focus on their behaviors. What do they do that makes them seem more confident? Maybe it's their posture, maybe it's their body language, maybe they have stronger levels of volume. Guess what you do after that? Start applying and practicing the behaviors you've noticed. Start copying it. Try it out. How about people in your circle or people online who are fun and playful? What do they do? As you watch them and as you experiment and you examine it, you might notice that, oh, people who are more fun and playful, they've got more melody in their voice. Maybe they're more humorous, maybe they smile a lot. Then try it. Start applying and practicing, emulate. Then again, with the third word that you chose or the third word that I chose, find someone in your circle who's smart. What do they do? Again, you might notice they have great articulation. They're always prepared. They speak really concisely. Start applying those things. Take a protest drive, emulate them. It doesn't have to be more complicated than this. If you met people, a group of strangers at a bar, when you leave that conversation, what three words do you want them to say about you? Let me know in the comment section what three words you'd want them to say. And look, if you're new and starting out your communication skills journey this year in 2024 and you're new to my content, come join me for a two-hour live free masterclass this February. To save your spot in my masterclass, check out the comment section. I'll make sure my team leaves a link there in the comment section for you to better register. Looking forward to seeing you in the masterclass.
